{
  "name": "@mms/system",
  "private": true,
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "tsc -b && vite build",
    "lint": "eslint .",
    "format": "prettier --write",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "db:generate": "drizzle-kit generate",
    "db:migrate": "drizzle-kit migrate",
    "db:push": "drizzle-kit push",
    "db:studio": "drizzle-kit studio",
    "test": "bun test",
    "test:unit": "bun test --grep -v 'integration'",
    "test:integration": "bun test src/**/*.integration.test.ts",
    "test:all": "bun test && bun run test:integration"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@belt/tools": "0.1.2",
    "@blueprintjs/core": "6.1.0",
    "@blueprintjs/icons": "6.1.0",
    "@deck.gl/aggregation-layers": "9.2.5",
    "@deck.gl/core": "9.2.5",

    "bullmq": "^5.34.8",
    "chokidar": "5.0.0",
    "date-fns": "^4.1.0",
    "date-fns-tz": "^3.2.0",
    "drizzle-orm": "^0.44.0",
    "exceljs": "4.4.0",
    "hono": "4.11.4",
    "ioredis": "^5.4.2",
    "konva": "10.2.0",
    "papaparse": "5.5.3",
    "pdfmake": "0.3.3",
    "pino": "^9.6.0",
    "postgres": "^3.4.5",
    "prom-client": "^15.1.3",
    "react": "18.3.1",
    "react-dom": "18.3.1",
    "react-konva": "18.2.14",
    "uuid": "13.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@eslint/js": "^9.39.1",
    "@types/bun": "1.3.6",
    "@types/chokidar": "2.1.7",
    "@types/node": "^24.10.1",
    "@types/papaparse": "5.5.2",
    "@types/react": "18.3.12",
    "@types/react-dom": "18.3.1",
    "@types/three": "0.170.0",
    "@types/uuid": "11.0.0",
    "@vitejs/plugin-react": "^5.1.1",

    "eslint": "^9.39.1",
    "eslint-plugin-react-hooks": "^7.0.1",
    "eslint-plugin-react-refresh": "^0.4.24",
    "globals": "^16.5.0",
    "typescript": "~5.9.3",

    "vite": "^7.2.4"
  }
}

1έΗΤ# MMS Development Docker Compose
     2έΗΤ#
     3έΗΤ# Starts all MMS services for local development.
     4έΗΤ#
     5έΗΤ# Usage:
     6έΗΤ#   docker compose up -d                   # Start all services
     7έΗΤ#   docker compose up -d postgres redis    # Start data services only
     8έΗΤ#   docker compose logs -f backend         # View backend logs
     9έΗΤ#   docker compose down -v                 # Stop and remove volumes
    10έΗΤ#
    11έΗΤ# Environment:
    12έΗΤ#   Copy .env.example to .env and configure as needed
    13έΗΤ#
    14έΗΤ# Services:
    15έΗΤ#   - postgres: PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB
    16έΗΤ#   - redis: Redis 7 for queues and caching
    17έΗΤ#   - backend: MMS API server (Bun + Hono)
    18έΗΤ#   - worker: MMS background workers (BullMQ)
    19έΗΤ#   - frontend: React development server (Vite)
    20έΗΤ#   - file-watcher: Mileage CSV file watcher
    21έΗΤ#   - optimizer: C++ OR-Tools optimizer binary
    22έΗΤ#
    23έΗΤ# @see specs/008-operations.md
    24έΗΤ
    25έΗΤname: mms-dev
    26έΗΤ
    27έΗΤservices:
    28έΗΤ  # =============================================================================
    29έΗΤ  # Data Layer
    30έΗΤ  # =============================================================================
    31έΗΤ
    32έΗΤ  postgres:
    33έΗΤ    image: timescale/timescaledb:2.14.2-pg16
    34έΗΤ    container_name: mms-postgres
    35έΗΤ    restart: unless-stopped
    36έΗΤ    ports:
    37έΗΤ      - "${POSTGRES_PORT:-5432}:5432"
    38έΗΤ    environment:
    39έΗΤ      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
    40έΗΤ      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}
    41έΗΤ      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}
    42έΗΤ    volumes:
    43έΗΤ      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
    44έΗΤ      - ./drizzle/manual:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    45έΗΤ    healthcheck:
    46έΗΤ      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-mms} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}"]
    47έΗΤ      interval: 10s
    48έΗΤ      timeout: 5s
    49έΗΤ      retries: 5
    50έΗΤ      start_period: 10s
    51έΗΤ    networks:
    52έΗΤ      - mms-network
    53έΗΤ
    54έΗΤ  redis:
    55έΗΤ    image: redis:7-alpine
    56έΗΤ    container_name: mms-redis
    57έΗΤ    restart: unless-stopped
    58έΗΤ    ports:
    59έΗΤ      - "${REDIS_PORT:-6379}:6379"
    60έΗΤ    command: >
    61έΗΤ      redis-server
    62έΗΤ      --appendonly yes
    63έΗΤ      --maxmemory 256mb
    64έΗΤ      --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    65έΗΤ    volumes:
    66έΗΤ      - redis_data:/data
    67έΗΤ    healthcheck:
    68έΗΤ      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
    69έΗΤ      interval: 10s
    70έΗΤ      timeout: 5s
    71έΗΤ      retries: 5
    72έΗΤ      start_period: 5s
    73έΗΤ    networks:
    74έΗΤ      - mms-network
    75έΗΤ
    76έΗΤ  # =============================================================================
    77έΗΤ  # Application Layer
    78έΗΤ  # =============================================================================
    79έΗΤ
    80έΗΤ  backend:
    81έΗΤ    build:
    82έΗΤ      context: .
    83έΗΤ      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.backend
    84έΗΤ    container_name: mms-backend
    85έΗΤ    restart: unless-stopped
    86έΗΤ    ports:
    87έΗΤ      - "${API_PORT:-3000}:3000"
    88έΗΤ    environment:
    89έΗΤ      NODE_ENV: development
    90έΗΤ      PORT: 3000
    91έΗΤ      HOST: 0.0.0.0
    92έΗΤ      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
    93έΗΤ      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
    94έΗΤ      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev_jwt_secret_change_in_production}
    95έΗΤ      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-debug}
    96έΗΤ      OTEL_ENABLED: ${OTEL_ENABLED:-false}
    97έΗΤ    depends_on:
    98έΗΤ      postgres:
    99έΗΤ        condition: service_healthy
   100έΗΤ      redis:
   101έΗΤ        condition: service_healthy
   102έΗΤ    healthcheck:
   103έΗΤ      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
   104έΗΤ      interval: 30s
   105έΗΤ      timeout: 5s
   106έΗΤ      retries: 3
   107έΗΤ      start_period: 10s
   108έΗΤ    networks:
   109έΗΤ      - mms-network
   110έΗΤ    develop:
   111έΗΤ      watch:
   112έΗΤ        - action: sync+restart
   113έΗΤ          path: ./src
   114έΗΤ          target: /app/src
   115έΗΤ
   116έΗΤ  worker:
   117έΗΤ    build:
   118έΗΤ      context: .
   119έΗΤ      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.worker
   120έΗΤ    container_name: mms-worker
   121έΗΤ    restart: unless-stopped
   122έΗΤ    environment:
   123έΗΤ      NODE_ENV: development
   124έΗΤ      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
   125έΗΤ      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
   126έΗΤ      REDIS_HOST: redis
   127έΗΤ      REDIS_PORT: 6379
   128έΗΤ      OPTIMIZER_BINARY_PATH: /opt/optimizer/mms-optimizer
   129έΗΤ      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-debug}
   130έΗΤ      WORKERS: ${WORKERS:-}
   131έΗΤ    depends_on:
   132έΗΤ      postgres:
   133έΗΤ        condition: service_healthy
   134έΗΤ      redis:
   135έΗΤ        condition: service_healthy
   136έΗΤ      optimizer:
   137έΗΤ        condition: service_started
   138έΗΤ    volumes:
   139έΗΤ      - optimizer_binary:/opt/optimizer:ro
   140έΗΤ    networks:
   141έΗΤ      - mms-network
   142έΗΤ    develop:
   143έΗΤ      watch:
   144έΗΤ        - action: sync+restart
   145έΗΤ          path: ./src
   146έΗΤ          target: /app/src
   147έΗΤ
   148έΗΤ  frontend:
   149έΗΤ    build:
   150έΗΤ      context: .
   151έΗΤ      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.frontend
   152έΗΤ      target: development
   153έΗΤ    container_name: mms-frontend
   154έΗΤ    restart: unless-stopped
   155έΗΤ    ports:
   156έΗΤ      - "${FRONTEND_PORT:-5173}:5173"
   157έΗΤ    environment:
   158έΗΤ      VITE_API_URL: http://localhost:${API_PORT:-3000}
   159έΗΤ    volumes:
   160έΗΤ      - ./src:/app/src:ro
   161έΗΤ      - ./public:/app/public:ro
   162έΗΤ    networks:
   163έΗΤ      - mms-network
   164έΗΤ
   165έΗΤ  file-watcher:
   166έΗΤ    build:
   167έΗΤ      context: .
   168έΗΤ      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.file-watcher
   169έΗΤ    container_name: mms-file-watcher
   170έΗΤ    restart: unless-stopped
   171έΗΤ    environment:
   172έΗΤ      NODE_ENV: development
   173έΗΤ      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
   174έΗΤ      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
   175έΗΤ      WATCH_PATH: /data/inbox
   176έΗΤ      QUARANTINE_PATH: /data/quarantine
   177έΗΤ      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-debug}
   178έΗΤ    depends_on:
   179έΗΤ      postgres:
   180έΗΤ        condition: service_healthy
   181έΗΤ      redis:
   182έΗΤ        condition: service_healthy
   183έΗΤ    volumes:
   184έΗΤ      - file_inbox:/data/inbox
   185έΗΤ      - file_quarantine:/data/quarantine
   186έΗΤ    networks:
   187έΗΤ      - mms-network
   188έΗΤ
   189έΗΤ  # =============================================================================
   190έΗΤ  # Optimizer Sidecar (C++ Binary)
   191έΗΤ  # =============================================================================
   192έΗΤ
   193έΗΤ  optimizer:
   194έΗΤ    build:
   195έΗΤ      context: ./mms-optimizer
   196έΗΤ      dockerfile: Dockerfile
   197έΗΤ    container_name: mms-optimizer
   198έΗΤ    # The optimizer binary is spawned by the worker process
   199έΗΤ    # This container just makes the binary available
   200έΗΤ    entrypoint: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
   201έΗΤ    command: ["cp /app/mms-optimizer /opt/optimizer/ && tail -f /dev/null"]
   202έΗΤ    volumes:
   203έΗΤ      - optimizer_binary:/opt/optimizer
   204έΗΤ    networks:
   205έΗΤ      - mms-network
   206έΗΤ
   207έΗΤ  # =============================================================================
   208έΗΤ  # Development Utilities
   209έΗΤ  # =============================================================================
   210έΗΤ
   211έΗΤ  # Redis Commander - Web UI for Redis
   212έΗΤ  redis-commander:
   213έΗΤ    image: rediscommander/redis-commander:latest
   214έΗΤ    container_name: mms-redis-commander
   215έΗΤ    restart: unless-stopped
   216έΗΤ    ports:
   217έΗΤ      - "${REDIS_COMMANDER_PORT:-8081}:8081"
   218έΗΤ    environment:
   219έΗΤ      REDIS_HOSTS: local:redis:6379
   220έΗΤ    depends_on:
   221έΗΤ      - redis
   222έΗΤ    networks:
   223έΗΤ      - mms-network
   224έΗΤ    profiles:
   225έΗΤ      - tools
   226έΗΤ
   227έΗΤ  # pgAdmin - Web UI for PostgreSQL
   228έΗΤ  pgadmin:
   229έΗΤ    image: dpage/pgadmin4:latest
   230έΗΤ    container_name: mms-pgadmin
   231έΗΤ    restart: unless-stopped
   232έΗΤ    ports:
   233έΗΤ      - "${PGADMIN_PORT:-8082}:80"
   234έΗΤ    environment:
   235έΗΤ      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL: ${PGADMIN_EMAIL:-admin@mms.local}
   236έΗΤ      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: ${PGADMIN_PASSWORD:-admin}
   237έΗΤ      PGADMIN_CONFIG_SERVER_MODE: "False"
   238έΗΤ    volumes:
   239έΗΤ      - pgadmin_data:/var/lib/pgadmin
   240έΗΤ    depends_on:
   241έΗΤ      - postgres
   242έΗΤ    networks:
   243έΗΤ      - mms-network
   244έΗΤ    profiles:
   245έΗΤ      - tools
   246έΗΤ
   247έΗΤ# =============================================================================
   248έΗΤ# Networks
   249έΗΤ# =============================================================================
   250έΗΤ
   251έΗΤnetworks:
   252έΗΤ  mms-network:
   253έΗΤ    driver: bridge
   254έΗΤ
   255έΗΤ# =============================================================================
   256έΗΤ# Volumes
   257έΗΤ# =============================================================================
   258έΗΤ

   260έΗΤ  postgres_data:
   261έΗΤ    driver: local

   263έΗΤ    driver: local

file.content:
# MMS Development Docker Compose
#
# Starts all MMS services for local development.
#
# Usage:
#   docker compose up -d                   # Start all services
#   docker compose up -d postgres redis    # Start data services only
#   docker compose logs -f backend         # View backend logs
#   docker compose down -v                 # Stop and remove volumes
#
# Environment:
#   Copy .env.example to .env and configure as needed
#
# Services:
#   - postgres: PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB
#   - redis: Redis 7 for queues and caching
#   - backend: MMS API server (Bun + Hono)
#   - worker: MMS background workers (BullMQ)
#   - frontend: React development server (Vite)
#   - file-watcher: Mileage CSV file watcher
#   - optimizer: C++ OR-Tools optimizer binary
#
# @see specs/008-operations.md

name: mms-dev

services:
  # =============================================================================
  # Data Layer
  # =============================================================================

  postgres:
    image: timescale/timescaledb:2.14.2-pg16
    container_name: mms-postgres
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${POSTGRES_PORT:-5432}:5432"
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
      POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}
    volumes:
      - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
      - ./drizzle/manual:/docker-entrypoint-initdb.d:ro
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER:-mms} -d ${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 10s
    networks:
      - mms-network

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    container_name: mms-redis
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${REDIS_PORT:-6379}:6379"
    command: >
      redis-server
      --appendonly yes
      --maxmemory 256mb
      --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
    volumes:
      - redis_data:/data
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "ping"]
      interval: 10s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 5s
    networks:
      - mms-network

  # =============================================================================
  # Application Layer
  # =============================================================================

  backend:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.backend
    container_name: mms-backend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${API_PORT:-3000}:3000"
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: development
      PORT: 3000
      HOST: 0.0.0.0
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      JWT_SECRET: ${JWT_SECRET:-dev_jwt_secret_change_in_production}
      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-debug}
      OTEL_ENABLED: ${OTEL_ENABLED:-false}
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "-q", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 10s
    networks:
      - mms-network
    develop:
      watch:
        - action: sync+restart
          path: ./src
          target: /app/src

  worker:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.worker
    container_name: mms-worker
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: development
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      REDIS_HOST: redis
      REDIS_PORT: 6379
      OPTIMIZER_BINARY_PATH: /opt/optimizer/mms-optimizer
      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-debug}
      WORKERS: ${WORKERS:-}
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
      optimizer:
        condition: service_started
    volumes:
      - optimizer_binary:/opt/optimizer:ro
    networks:
      - mms-network
    develop:
      watch:
        - action: sync+restart
          path: ./src
          target: /app/src

  frontend:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.frontend
      target: development
    container_name: mms-frontend
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${FRONTEND_PORT:-5173}:5173"
    environment:
      VITE_API_URL: http://localhost:${API_PORT:-3000}
    volumes:
      - ./src:/app/src:ro
      - ./public:/app/public:ro
    networks:
      - mms-network

  file-watcher:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: docker/Dockerfile.file-watcher
    container_name: mms-file-watcher
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      NODE_ENV: development
      DATABASE_URL: postgres://${POSTGRES_USER:-mms}:${POSTGRES_PASSWORD:-mms_dev_password}@postgres:5432/${POSTGRES_DB:-mms}
      REDIS_URL: redis://redis:6379
      WATCH_PATH: /data/inbox
      QUARANTINE_PATH: /data/quarantine
      LOG_LEVEL: ${LOG_LEVEL:-debug}
    depends_on:
      postgres:
        condition: service_healthy
      redis:
        condition: service_healthy
    volumes:
      - file_inbox:/data/inbox
      - file_quarantine:/data/quarantine
    networks:
      - mms-network

  # =============================================================================
  # Optimizer Sidecar (C++ Binary)
  # =============================================================================

  optimizer:
    build:
      context: ./mms-optimizer
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    container_name: mms-optimizer
    # The optimizer binary is spawned by the worker process
    # This container just makes the binary available
    entrypoint: ["/bin/sh", "-c"]
    command: ["cp /app/mms-optimizer /opt/optimizer/ && tail -f /dev/null"]
    volumes:
      - optimizer_binary:/opt/optimizer
    networks:
      - mms-network

  # =============================================================================
  # Development Utilities
  # =============================================================================

  # Redis Commander - Web UI for Redis
  redis-commander:
    image: rediscommander/redis-commander:latest
    container_name: mms-redis-commander
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${REDIS_COMMANDER_PORT:-8081}:8081"
    environment:
      REDIS_HOSTS: local:redis:6379
    depends_on:
      - redis
    networks:
      - mms-network
    profiles:
      - tools

  # pgAdmin - Web UI for PostgreSQL
  pgadmin:
    image: dpage/pgadmin4:latest
    container_name: mms-pgadmin
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "${PGADMIN_PORT:-8082}:80"
    environment:
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_EMAIL: ${PGADMIN_EMAIL:-admin@mms.local}
      PGADMIN_DEFAULT_PASSWORD: ${PGADMIN_PASSWORD:-admin}
      PGADMIN_CONFIG_SERVER_MODE: "False"
    volumes:
      - pgadmin_data:/var/lib/pgadmin
    depends_on:
      - postgres
    networks:
      - mms-network
    profiles:
      - tools

# =============================================================================
# Networks
# =============================================================================

networks:
  mms-network:
    driver: bridge

# =============================================================================
# Volumes
# =============================================================================

volumes:
  postgres_data:
    driver: local
  redis_data:
    driver: local
  optimizer_binary:
    driver: local
  file_inbox:
    driver: local
  file_quarantine:
    driver: local
  pgadmin_data:
    driver: local

content.content:
1έΗΤ# MMS Demo Application 2έΗΤ 3έΗΤ**Metro Fleet Mileage Management System** - Win-the-bid demonstration application. 4έΗΤ 5έΗΤ## Overview 6έΗΤ 7έΗΤThis is a demonstration application for Metro fleet preventive maintenance (PM) management. It showcases CSV-based PM projection over a 10-year horizon with an infinite canvas interface using semantic zoom levels (strategic έΗΤ tactical έΗΤ operational views). 8έΗΤ 9έΗΤ**Key Features:** 10έΗΤ- Drag-and-drop PM rescheduling with constraint validation 11έΗΤ- "Bow-wave" risk visualization (fleet maintenance clustering) 12έΗΤ- WebGL-powered infinite canvas rendering with deck.gl 13έΗΤ- State management with @belt/tools 14έΗΤ 15έΗΤ## Getting Started 16έΗΤ 17έΗΤ```bash 18έΗΤ# Install dependencies 19έΗΤbun install 20έΗΤ 21έΗΤ# Start development server 22έΗΤbun dev 23έΗΤ 24έΗΤ# Build for production 25έΗΤbun run build 26έΗΤ 27έΗΤ# Lint code 28έΗΤbun run lint 29έΗΤ 30έΗΤ# Format code 31έΗΤbun run format 32έΗΤ``` 33έΗΤ 34έΗΤ## Project Structure 35έΗΤ 36έΗΤ``` 37έΗΤsrc/ 38έΗΤέΦεέΦΑέΦΑ main.tsx # Entry point 39έΗΤέΦεέΦΑέΦΑ App.tsx # Root component 40έΗΤέΦεέΦΑέΦΑ store/ # @belt/tools state machines 41έΗΤέΦεέΦΑέΦΑ engine/ # PM projection algorithm (CRITICAL) 42έΗΤέΦεέΦΑέΦΑ import/ # CSV parsing and validation 43έΗΤέΦεέΦΑέΦΑ canvas/ # deck.gl infinite canvas 44έΗΤέΦεέΦΑέΦΑ components/ # React UI components 45έΗΤέΦεέΦΑέΦΑ types/ # TypeScript interfaces 46έΗΤέΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ utils/ # Helpers 47έΗΤ 48έΗΤpublic/sample-data/ # Sample CSV files for testing 49έΗΤdocs/ # 9-part specification (MMS_SPEC_001-009) 50έΗΤ``` 51έΗΤ 52έΗΤ--- 53έΗΤ 54έΗΤ## @belt/tools Utilities Reference 55έΗΤ 56έΗΤThe `@belt/tools` package provides comprehensive utilities for state management, runtime validation, and common operations. All utilities are already available in this project. 57έΗΤ 58έΗΤ### State Machine 59έΗΤ 60έΗΤImmer-based reactive state management with React hooks and automatic cache management. 61έΗΤ 62έΗΤ#### Creating Slices 63έΗΤ 64έΗΤ```typescript 65έΗΤimport { createSlice, createAsyncSlice, createMachine } from '@belt/tools'; 66έΗΤ 67έΗΤ// Synchronous slice for local state 68έΗΤconst fleetSlice = createSlice({ 69έΗΤ id: 'fleet', 70έΗΤ initial: { 71έΗΤ trains: [], 72έΗΤ pmHistory: [], 73έΗΤ selectedTrain: null as string | null, 74έΗΤ }, 75έΗΤ cache: { policy: 'session' }, // or 'public', 'private' 76έΗΤ reducers: { 77έΗΤ importFleet: (state, payload: Train[]) => { 78έΗΤ state.trains = payload; // Immer allows direct mutations 79έΗΤ }, 80έΗΤ selectTrain: (state, trainId: string) => { 81έΗΤ state.selectedTrain = trainId; 82έΗΤ }, 83έΗΤ clearSelection: (state) => { 84έΗΤ state.selectedTrain = null; 85έΗΤ }, 86έΗΤ }, 87έΗΤ}); 88έΗΤ 89έΗΤ// Async slice for data fetching 90έΗΤconst analyticsSlice = createAsyncSlice({ 91έΗΤ id: 'analytics', 92έΗΤ cache: { 93έΗΤ policy: 'public', 94έΗΤ ttl: '5m', // Time-to-live using ms() format 95έΗΤ swr: true, // Stale-while-revalidate 96έΗΤ }, 97έΗΤ context: {}, 98έΗΤ load: async () => { 99έΗΤ const response = await fetch('/api/analytics'); 100έΗΤ return response.json(); 101έΗΤ }, 102έΗΤ}); 103έΗΤ``` 104έΗΤ 105έΗΤ#### Creating Machines 106έΗΤ 107έΗΤ```tsx 108έΗΤimport { createMachine } from '@belt/tools'; 109έΗΤ 110έΗΤconst appMachine = createMachine({ 111έΗΤ id: 'mms', 112έΗΤ slices: { 113έΗΤ fleet: fleetSlice, 114έΗΤ analytics: analyticsSlice, 115έΗΤ }, 116έΗΤ context: { userId: null }, // Global context 117έΗΤ}); 118έΗΤ 119έΗΤ// Use in React components 120έΗΤfunction FleetView() { 121έΗΤ const { state, actions } = appMachine.useMachine('fleet'); 122έΗΤ 123έΗΤ return ( 124έΗΤ
125έΗΤ 128έΗΤ
{state.trains.length} trains loaded
129έΗΤ
130έΗΤ ); 131έΗΤ} 132έΗΤ``` 133έΗΤ 134έΗΤ**Cache Policies:** 135έΗΤ- `public` - Shared cache, TTL-based invalidation 136έΗΤ- `private` - User-specific cache, invalidated on user change 137έΗΤ- `session` - Session-specific cache, invalidated on app restart 138έΗΤ 139έΗΤ--- 140έΗΤ 141έΗΤ### Runtime Schema Validation 142έΗΤ 143έΗΤType-safe runtime validation using TypeBox with concise syntax. 144έΗΤ 145έΗΤ#### Basic Types 146έΗΤ 147έΗΤ```typescript 148έΗΤimport { createType, createSchema, isType, invariantType, InferType } from '@belt/tools'; 149έΗΤ 150έΗΤ// Primitive types 151έΗΤconst StringType = createType('string'); 152έΗΤconst NumberType = createType('number'); 153έΗΤconst BooleanType = createType('boolean'); 154έΗΤconst DateType = createType('date'); 155έΗΤ 156έΗΤ// Optional types (append ?) 157έΗΤconst OptionalString = createType('string?'); 158έΗΤconst OptionalNumber = createType('number?'); 159έΗΤ 160έΗΤ// Array types (append []) 161έΗΤconst StringArray = createType('string[]'); 162έΗΤconst NumberArray = createType('number[]'); 163έΗΤ 164έΗΤ// Type guards 165έΗΤif (isType(value, 'string')) { 166έΗΤ console.log(value.toUpperCase()); // TypeScript knows it's string 167έΗΤ} 168έΗΤ 169έΗΤif (isType(value, 'date')) { 170έΗΤ console.log(value.toISOString()); // TypeScript knows it's Date 171έΗΤ} 172έΗΤ``` 173έΗΤ 174έΗΤ#### Object Schemas 175έΗΤ 176έΗΤ```typescript 177έΗΤimport { createSchema, InferType } from '@belt/tools'; 178έΗΤ 179έΗΤ// Define schema 180έΗΤconst TrainModel = createSchema({ 181έΗΤ id: 'string', 182έΗΤ name: 'string', 183έΗΤ mileage: 'number', 184έΗΤ lastPM: 'date', 185έΗΤ tags: 'string[]', 186έΗΤ metadata: 'object?', // Optional generic object 187έΗΤ}); 188έΗΤ 189έΗΤ// Infer TypeScript type 190έΗΤtype Train = InferType; 191έΗΤ 192έΗΤ// Parse and validate 193έΗΤconst train = TrainModel.parse({ 194έΗΤ id: 'T001', 195έΗΤ name: 'Red Line', 196έΗΤ mileage: 50000, 197έΗΤ lastPM: new Date('2025-01-01'), 198έΗΤ tags: ['metro', 'active'], 199έΗΤ}); 200έΗΤ 201έΗΤ// Validate without parsing 202έΗΤif (TrainModel.isValid(data)) { 203έΗΤ // TypeScript knows data is Train 204έΗΤ} 205έΗΤ 206έΗΤ// Assert type (throws if invalid) 207έΗΤinvariantType(TrainModel, data); 208έΗΤ``` 209έΗΤ 210έΗΤ#### Complex Types 211έΗΤ 212έΗΤ```typescript 213έΗΤimport { createType } from '@belt/tools'; 214έΗΤ 215έΗΤ// Nested objects 216έΗΤconst PMEventType = createType({ 217έΗΤ properties: { 218έΗΤ trainId: 'string', 219έΗΤ scheduledDate: 'date', 220έΗΤ location: { 221έΗΤ properties: { 222έΗΤ facility: 'string', 223έΗΤ bay: 'number', 224έΗΤ }, 225έΗΤ }, 226έΗΤ tasks: { 227έΗΤ items: { // Array of objects 228έΗΤ properties: { 229έΗΤ code: 'string', 230έΗΤ duration: 'number', 231έΗΤ completed: 'boolean?', 232έΗΤ }, 233έΗΤ }, 234έΗΤ }, 235έΗΤ }, 236έΗΤ}); 237έΗΤ 238έΗΤ// Enums 239έΗΤconst StatusEnum = createType({ 240έΗΤ enum: ['pending', 'in-progress', 'completed', 'cancelled'], 241έΗΤ}); 242έΗΤ 243έΗΤ// Unions 244έΗΤconst IDType = createType({ 245έΗΤ union: ['string', 'number'], 246έΗΤ}); 247έΗΤ 248έΗΤ// Records (key-value maps) 249έΗΤconst ConfigType = createType({ 250έΗΤ record: 'string', // Record 251έΗΤ}); 252έΗΤ``` 253έΗΤ 254έΗΤ#### List Parsing 255έΗΤ 256έΗΤ```typescript 257έΗΤ// Parse array of items 258έΗΤconst trainList = TrainModel.list().parse([ 259έΗΤ { id: 'T001', name: 'Red Line', mileage: 50000, lastPM: new Date(), tags: [] }, 260έΗΤ { id: 'T002', name: 'Blue Line', mileage: 30000, lastPM: new Date(), tags: [] }, 261έΗΤ]); 262έΗΤ 263έΗΤ// Parse array from nested path 264έΗΤconst events = PMEventType.parseList(response, 'data.events'); 265έΗΤ``` 266έΗΤ 267έΗΤ--- 268έΗΤ 269έΗΤ### Date Utilities 270έΗΤ 271έΗΤDate manipulation and formatting using date-fns. 272έΗΤ 273έΗΤ```typescript 274έΗΤimport { formatDate, setDefaultDateLocale, ms } from '@belt/tools'; 275έΗΤimport { ptBR } from 'date-fns/locale'; 276έΗΤ 277έΗΤ// Format dates 278έΗΤformatDate(new Date(), 'yyyy-MM-dd'); // '2026-01-14' 279έΗΤformatDate(new Date(), 'MMM dd, yyyy'); // 'Jan 14, 2026' 280έΗΤformatDate('2026-01-14', 'dd/MM/yyyy'); // '14/01/2026' 281έΗΤ 282έΗΤ// Set global locale 283έΗΤsetDefaultDateLocale(ptBR); 284έΗΤformatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM'); // 'janeiro' 285έΗΤ 286έΗΤ// Override locale for single call 287έΗΤimport { enUS } from 'date-fns/locale'; 288έΗΤformatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM', { locale: enUS }); // 'January' 289έΗΤ 290έΗΤ// Time string conversion 291έΗΤms('1s'); // 1000 292έΗΤms('5m'); // 300000 293έΗΤms('1h'); // 3600000 294έΗΤms('2d'); // 172800000 295έΗΤms('1w'); // 604800000 296έΗΤms('1mo'); // ~2629800000 297έΗΤms('1y'); // ~31557600000 298έΗΤms('500ms'); // 500 299έΗΤ 300έΗΤ// Use in configs 301έΗΤconst CACHE_TTL = ms('5m'); 302έΗΤconst DEBOUNCE = ms('300ms'); 303έΗΤconst POLL_INTERVAL = ms('30s'); 304έΗΤ``` 305έΗΤ 306έΗΤ**Supported time units:** `ms`, `s`, `m`/`min`, `h`, `d`, `w`, `mo`/`mon`/`months`, `y` 307έΗΤ 308έΗΤ--- 309έΗΤ 310έΗΤ### Object Utilities 311έΗΤ 312έΗΤType-safe object manipulation with deep path access. 313έΗΤ 314έΗΤ```typescript 315έΗΤimport { 316έΗΤ getProperty, 317έΗΤ setProperty, 318έΗΤ objectKeys, 319έΗΤ objectEntries, 320έΗΤ objectValues, 321έΗΤ keyBy, 322έΗΤ isObject, 323έΗΤ assertObject, 324έΗΤ requireAll, 325έΗΤ deepFreeze, 326έΗΤ} from '@belt/tools'; 327έΗΤ 328έΗΤ// Deep property access (type-safe) 329έΗΤconst street = getProperty(user, 'address.street'); 330έΗΤconst city = getProperty(user, 'address.city', true); // Allow falsy values 331έΗΤ 332έΗΤ// Set nested properties 333έΗΤsetProperty(train, 'metadata.lastInspection', new Date()); 334έΗΤ 335έΗΤ// Object manipulation 336έΗΤconst keys = objectKeys(train); // Type-safe keys 337έΗΤconst entries = objectEntries(train); // Typed entries 338έΗΤconst values = objectValues(train); // Typed values 339έΗΤ 340έΗΤ// Array to object by key 341έΗΤconst trainsById = keyBy(trains, 'id'); 342έΗΤ// Or with function 343έΗΤconst trainsByName = keyBy(trains, (t) => t.name); 344έΗΤ 345έΗΤ// Type guards 346έΗΤif (isObject(value)) { 347έΗΤ // value is UnknownRecord 348έΗΤ} 349έΗΤ 350έΗΤ// Assert object with no falsy values 351έΗΤconst validated = requireAll({ 352έΗΤ id: train.id, 353έΗΤ name: train.name, 354έΗΤ mileage: train.mileage, 355έΗΤ}); 356έΗΤ 357έΗΤ// Deep freeze (immutability) 358έΗΤconst frozen = deepFreeze(config); 359έΗΤ``` 360έΗΤ 361έΗΤ**Path Examples:** 362έΗΤ```typescript 363έΗΤtype User = { 364έΗΤ id: string; 365έΗΤ profile: { 366έΗΤ address: { 367έΗΤ street: string; 368έΗΤ city: string; 369έΗΤ }; 370έΗΤ }; 371έΗΤ}; 372έΗΤ 373έΗΤ// All paths are type-checked 374έΗΤgetProperty(user, 'id'); // έεΥ string 375έΗΤgetProperty(user, 'profile.address.city'); // έεΥ string 376έΗΤgetProperty(user, 'profile.invalid'); // έεΩ Type error 377έΗΤ``` 378έΗΤ 379έΗΤ--- 380έΗΤ 381έΗΤ### String Utilities 382έΗΤ 383έΗΤString manipulation with type inference. 384έΗΤ 385έΗΤ```typescript 386έΗΤimport { capitalize, isString, assertString } from '@belt/tools'; 387έΗΤ 388έΗΤ// Capitalize 389έΗΤcapitalize('hello'); // 'Hello' (type: 'Hello') 390έΗΤ 391έΗΤ// Type guards 392έΗΤif (isString(value)) { 393έΗΤ console.log(value.toUpperCase()); 394έΗΤ} 395έΗΤ 396έΗΤ// Assertions 397έΗΤassertString(value); 398έΗΤassertString(value, { notEmpty: true }); 399έΗΤassertString(value, { message: 'Name is required', notEmpty: true }); 400έΗΤ``` 401έΗΤ 402έΗΤ--- 403έΗΤ 404έΗΤ### Error Handling 405έΗΤ 406έΗΤComprehensive error utilities with stack trace management. 407έΗΤ 408έΗΤ```typescript 409έΗΤimport { 410έΗΤ invariant, 411έΗΤ tryCatch, 412έΗΤ InvariantError, 413έΗΤ createErrorList, 414έΗΤ} from '@belt/tools'; 415έΗΤ 416έΗΤ// Assertions 417έΗΤinvariant(user.id, 'User ID is required'); 418έΗΤinvariant(trains.length > 0, 'No trains found'); 419έΗΤ 420έΗΤ// Safe error handling 421έΗΤconst [error, result] = tryCatch(() => JSON.parse(data)); 422έΗΤif (error) { 423έΗΤ console.error('Parse failed:', error.message); 424έΗΤ} else { 425έΗΤ console.log('Success:', result); 426έΗΤ} 427έΗΤ 428έΗΤ// Collect multiple errors 429έΗΤconst errors = createErrorList('Validation failed: ');     23έΗΤ## System Overview
    24έΗΤ
    25έΗΤMMS is a full-stack application designed to manage preventive maintenance (PM) scheduling for metro train fleets. The system optimizes train-to-route assignments, tracks mileage, projects maintenance needs over a 12-year horizon, and provides an interactive canvas for planners to visualize and adjust schedules.
    26έΗΤ
    27έΗΤ### Key Capabilities
    28έΗΤ
    29έΗΤ- **Mileage Tracking**: Ingest daily mileage readings from CSV files, detect anomalies, handle odometer events
    30έΗΤ- **PM Scheduling**: Schedule preventive maintenance within compliance windows (C1έΗΤC2έΗΤC3έΗΤC4έΗΤOverhaul hierarchy)
    31έΗΤ- **Run Assignment Optimization**: Assign trains to routes balancing mileage across the fleet (C++ OR-Tools CP-SAT solver)
    32έΗΤ- **Strategic Planning**: 10-year maintenance projection using genetic algorithms
    33έΗΤ- **Man-hour Leveling**: Balance workforce across depots and time periods
    34έΗΤ- **Drift Analysis**: Compare actual vs. baseline to detect schedule deviation
    35έΗΤ- **Interactive Canvas**: WebGL-powered infinite canvas with semantic zoom (strategicέΗΤtacticalέΗΤoperational views)
    36έΗΤ

    39έΗΤ## Technology Stack
    40έΗΤ
    41έΗΤ| Layer | Technology |
    42έΗΤ|-------|------------|
    43έΗΤ| **Runtime** | Bun 1.x |
    44έΗΤ| **Frontend** | React 18, Vite 7, deck.gl 9, Konva, BlueprintJS v6 |
    45έΗΤ| **Backend** | Hono 4.x (REST API + WebSocket) |
    46έΗΤ| **Database** | PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB 2.14 |
    47έΗΤ| **ORM** | Drizzle 0.44 |
    48έΗΤ| **Queue** | BullMQ 5.x (Redis-backed) |
    49έΗΤ| **Cache/Pub-Sub** | Redis 7 |
    50έΗΤ| **Optimizer** | C++ with OR-Tools CP-SAT (TypeScript fallback) |
    51έΗΤ| **Validation** | @belt/tools (TypeBox-based schemas) |
    52έΗΤ| **State Management** | @belt/tools machines (Immer-based) |
    53έΗΤ| **Observability** | OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Pino logging |
    54έΗΤ| **Containerization** | Docker, Docker Compose |

   118έΗΤ## Development Setup
   119έΗΤ
   120έΗΤ### Prerequisites
   121έΗΤ
   122έΗΤ- **Bun** 1.x (`curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash`)
   123έΗΤ- **Docker** & Docker Compose
   124έΗΤ- **Node.js** 20+ (for some tooling)
   125έΗΤ- **CMake** + OR-Tools (for C++ optimizer, optional)
   126έΗΤ
   127έΗΤ### Quick Start
   128έΗΤ
   129έΗΤ```bash
   130έΗΤ# Clone and install
   131έΗΤgit clone <repository>
   132έΗΤcd mms/system
   133έΗΤbun install
   134έΗΤ
   135έΗΤ# Copy environment
   136έΗΤcp .env.example .env
   137έΗΤ```
   138έΗΤ
   139έΗΤ### Option 1: Hybrid Development (Recommended)
   140έΗΤ
   141έΗΤRun data services in Docker, application locally for hot-reload:
   142έΗΤ
   143έΗΤ```bash
   144έΗΤ# Terminal 1: Start data services
   145έΗΤdocker compose up -d postgres redis
   146έΗΤ
   147έΗΤ# Terminal 2: Run database migrations
   148έΗΤbun run db:migrate
   149έΗΤ
   150έΗΤ# Terminal 3: Start frontend (Vite dev server)
   151έΗΤbun run dev
   152έΗΤ# έΗΤ http://localhost:5173
   153έΗΤ
   154έΗΤ# Terminal 4: Start backend (Hono API)
   155έΗΤbun run src/api/server.ts
   156έΗΤ# έΗΤ http://localhost:3000
   157έΗΤ
   158έΗΤ# Terminal 5: Start workers (optional)
   159έΗΤbun run src/workers/runner.ts
   160έΗΤ```
   161έΗΤ
   162έΗΤ### Option 2: Full Docker Stack
   163έΗΤ
   164έΗΤRun everything in containers:
   165έΗΤ
   166έΗΤ```bash
   167έΗΤ# Start all services
   168έΗΤdocker compose up -d
   169έΗΤ
   170έΗΤ# With dev tools (pgAdmin, Redis Commander)
   171έΗΤdocker compose --profile tools up -d
   172έΗΤ
   173έΗΤ# View logs
   174έΗΤdocker compose logs -f backend
   175έΗΤdocker compose logs -f worker
   176έΗΤ```
   177έΗΤ
   178έΗΤ### Option 3: Minimal (Frontend Only)
   179έΗΤ
   180έΗΤFor UI development without backend:
   181έΗΤ
   182έΗΤ```bash
   183έΗΤbun run dev
   184έΗΤ# Mock API responses or use storybook
   185έΗΤ```
   186έΗΤ
   187έΗΤ### Environment Variables
   188έΗΤ
   189έΗΤ| Variable | Default | Description |
   190έΗΤ|----------|---------|-------------|
   191έΗΤ| `POSTGRES_DB` | mms | Database name |
   192έΗΤ| `POSTGRES_USER` | mms | Database user |
   193έΗΤ| `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` | mms_dev_password | Database password |
   194έΗΤ| `POSTGRES_PORT` | 5432 | PostgreSQL port |
   195έΗΤ| `REDIS_PORT` | 6379 | Redis port |
   196έΗΤ| `API_PORT` | 3000 | Backend API port |
   197έΗΤ| `FRONTEND_PORT` | 5173 | Vite dev server port |
   198έΗΤ| `JWT_SECRET` | dev_jwt_secret... | JWT signing key |
   199έΗΤ| `LOG_LEVEL` | debug | Logging verbosity |
   200έΗΤ| `OTEL_ENABLED` | false | OpenTelemetry tracing |
   201έΗΤ| `WORKERS` | (all) | Comma-separated worker list |
   202έΗΤ
   203έΗΤ### Database Commands
   204έΗΤ
   205έΗΤ```bash
   206έΗΤbun run db:generate    # Generate migration files from schema
   207έΗΤbun run db:migrate     # Run pending migrations
   208έΗΤbun run db:push        # Push schema directly (dev only)
   209έΗΤbun run db:studio      # Open Drizzle Studio at localhost:4983
   210έΗΤbun run db:seed        # Seed demo data
   211έΗΤ```

   213έΗΤ### C++ Optimizer Build (Optional)
   214έΗΤ
   215έΗΤ```bash
   216έΗΤ# macOS prerequisites
   217έΗΤbrew install cmake or-tools nlohmann-json fmt
   218έΗΤ
   219έΗΤ# Build
   220έΗΤcd mms-optimizer
   221έΗΤcmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
   222έΗΤcmake --build build

   230έΗΤ
   231έΗΤ### Validation Commands (Run in Order)
   232έΗΤ
   233έΗΤ```bash
   234έΗΤbun test <path>        # 1. Unit tests for changed files
   235έΗΤbun run typecheck      # 2. TypeScript strict mode
   236έΗΤbun run lint           # 3. ESLint
   237έΗΤbun run build          # 4. Full production build
   238έΗΤPORT=0 bun run test:integration  # 5. Integration tests

   240έΗΤ
   241έΗΤ**All must pass before committing.**

   244έΗΤ
   245έΗΤ## Deployment
   246έΗΤ
   247έΗΤ### Docker Images
   248έΗΤ
   249έΗΤ| Service | Dockerfile | Port | Description |
   250έΗΤ|---------|------------|------|-------------|
   251έΗΤ| backend | `docker/Dockerfile.backend` | 3000 | Hono API server |
   252έΗΤ| frontend | `docker/Dockerfile.frontend` | 80 | React SPA (Nginx) |
   253έΗΤ| worker | `docker/Dockerfile.worker` | - | BullMQ processors |
   254έΗΤ| file-watcher | `docker/Dockerfile.file-watcher` | - | CSV ingestion |
   255έΗΤ| optimizer | `mms-optimizer/Dockerfile` | - | C++ binary |
   256έΗΤ
   257έΗΤ### Production Build
   258έΗΤ
   259έΗΤ```bash
   260έΗΤ# Build all images
   261έΗΤdocker compose build
   262έΗΤ
   263έΗΤ# Or individual
   264έΗΤdocker build -f docker/Dockerfile.backend -t mms-backend .
   265έΗΤdocker build -f docker/Dockerfile.frontend -t mms-frontend .
   266έΗΤ```
   267έΗΤ
   268έΗΤ### Container Configuration
   269έΗΤ
   270έΗΤAll containers:
   271έΗΤ- Run as non-root user
   272έΗΤ- Include health checks
   273έΗΤ- Use multi-stage builds for minimal size
   274έΗΤ- Target sizes: backend <200MB, frontend <50MB
   275έΗΤ
   276έΗΤ### Docker Compose Services
   277έΗΤ
   278έΗΤ```yaml
   279έΗΤservices:
   280έΗΤ  postgres:     # PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB
   281έΗΤ  redis:        # Redis 7 Alpine
   282έΗΤ  backend:      # Hono API
   283έΗΤ  frontend:     # React/Vite (dev) or Nginx (prod)
   284έΗΤ  worker:       # BullMQ processors
   285έΗΤ  file-watcher: # CSV directory watcher
   286έΗΤ  optimizer:    # C++ OR-Tools binary
   287έΗΤ```
   288έΗΤ
   289έΗΤ### Volumes
   290έΗΤ
   291έΗΤ- `postgres_data` - Database persistence
   292έΗΤ- `redis_data` - Redis persistence
   293έΗΤ- `optimizer_binary` - Shared optimizer binary
   294έΗΤ- `file_inbox` / `file_quarantine` - CSV processing
   295έΗΤ
   296έΗΤ### Network
   297έΗΤ
   298έΗΤAll services communicate via `mms-network` bridge network.
   299έΗΤ

   380έΗΤ## Data Flow
   381έΗΤ
   382έΗΤ### Mileage Ingestion
   383έΗΤ
   384έΗΤ```
   385έΗΤCSV File Drop έΗΤ File Watcher έΗΤ Validation έΗΤ Queue (ingestion)
   386έΗΤ                                               έΦΓ
   387έΗΤ                                               έΨ╝
   388έΗΤ                              Parse έΗΤ Anomaly Detection έΗΤ mileage_readings
   389έΗΤ                                               έΦΓ
   390έΗΤ                                               έΨ╝
   391έΗΤ                              TimescaleDB έΗΤ Continuous Aggregates
   392έΗΤ```
   393έΗΤ
   394έΗΤ### PM Scheduling
   395έΗΤ
   396έΗΤ```
   397έΗΤUser Request έΗΤ API έΗΤ Queue (tactical_scheduler)
   398έΗΤ                           έΦΓ
   399έΗΤ                           έΨ╝
   400έΗΤ         Load Constraints έΗΤ Build Model έΗΤ Solve (OR-Tools or TS)
   401έΗΤ                           έΦΓ
   402έΗΤ                           έΨ╝
   403έΗΤ         pm_schedule έΗΤ WebSocket Broadcast έΗΤ UI Update
   404έΗΤ```
   405έΗΤ
   406έΗΤ### Drift Analysis
   407έΗΤ
   408έΗΤ```
   409έΗΤNightly Job έΗΤ Load Baseline έΗΤ Load Actual έΗΤ Compare
   410έΗΤ                                              έΦΓ
   411έΗΤ                                              έΨ╝
   412έΗΤ                              drift_analysis έΗΤ Flag Deviations
   413έΗΤ                                              έΦΓ
   414έΗΤ                                              έΨ╝
   415έΗΤ                              Notification έΗΤ Planner Alert
   416έΗΤ```
   417έΗΤ

   419έΗΤ
   420έΗΤ## Domain Model
   421έΗΤ
   422έΗΤ### Core Entities
   423έΗΤ
   424έΗΤ| Entity | Description |
   425έΗΤ|--------|-------------|
   426έΗΤ| **Fleet** | Group of trains (KS: Kinki Sharyo, ALSTOM: Metropolis) |
   427έΗΤ| **Train** | Individual vehicle with mileage, fitness status |
   428έΗΤ| **Depot** | Maintenance facility with tracks |
   429έΗΤ| **Track** | Physical location with capability (PIT, CRANE, etc.) |
   430έΗΤ| **Route** | Operational route with daily mileage profile |
   431έΗΤ| **PM Type** | Maintenance type (C1/C2/C3/C4/Overhaul) with intervals |
   432έΗΤ| **PM Schedule** | Scheduled maintenance event with compliance window |
   433έΗΤ| **Baseline** | Immutable snapshot for drift comparison |
   434έΗΤ| **Scenario** | What-if fork with delta events |
   435έΗΤ
   436έΗΤ### PM Hierarchy
   437έΗΤ
   438έΗΤ```
   439έΗΤC1 (6,000 km / 3 months)
   440έΗΤ έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ C2 (24,000 km / 6 months) έΦΑ satisfies C1
   441έΗΤ      έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ C3 (50,000 km / 12 months) έΦΑ satisfies C1, C2
   442έΗΤ           έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ C4 (200,000 km / 48 months) έΦΑ satisfies C1, C2, C3
   443έΗΤ                έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ Overhaul (1,000,000 km / 144 months) έΦΑ satisfies all
   444έΗΤ```
   445έΗΤ
   446έΗΤ### Compliance Windows
   447έΗΤ
   448έΗΤ- **Early tolerance**: 10% before due date
   449έΗΤ- **Late tolerance**: 10% after due date
   450έΗΤ- **Hard deadline**: Statutory limit (train grounded if exceeded)
   451έΗΤ
   452έΗΤ### Constraint Types
   453έΗΤ
   454έΗΤ1. **Hard constraints** (NEVER violate): Safety limits, physical impossibilities
   455έΗΤ2. **Soft constraints** (violate with penalty): Compliance windows, preferences
   456έΗΤ3. **Material constraints**: Inventory availability
   457έΗΤ4. **Track constraints**: Capability matching
   458έΗΤ
   459έΗΤ---
   460έΗΤ
   461έΗΤ## Integration Points
   462έΗΤ
   463έΗΤ### Maximo (CMMS)
   464έΗΤ
   465έΗΤ- **Direction**: Bidirectional
   466έΗΤ- **Protocol**: REST API (OSLC)
   467έΗΤ- **Receives**: Corrective maintenance (CM) status, work order updates
   468έΗΤ- **Sends**: PM work order requests
   469έΗΤ- **Boundary**: MMS never manages CM work orders (Maximo-owned)
   470έΗΤ
   471έΗΤ### File Inbox (Mileage CSV)
   472έΗΤ
   473έΗΤ- **Direction**: Inbound only
   474έΗΤ- **Protocol**: Filesystem watch
   475έΗΤ- **Format**: CSV with train ID, date, mileage
   476έΗΤ- **Flow**: inbox έΗΤ processing έΗΤ archive/quarantine
   477έΗΤ
   478έΗΤ### Inventory System
   479έΗΤ
   480έΗΤ- **Direction**: Inbound only
   481έΗΤ- **Protocol**: Snapshot consumption
   482έΗΤ- **Data**: Material availability, reservation status
   483έΗΤ
   484έΗΤ---
   485έΗΤ
   486έΗΤ## Observability
   487έΗΤ
   488έΗΤ### Logging
   489έΗΤ
   490έΗΤ- **Library**: Pino (structured JSON)
   491έΗΤ- **Levels**: debug, info, warn, error
   492έΗΤ- **Context**: Request ID, user ID, trace ID
   493έΗΤ
   494έΗΤ### Tracing
   495έΗΤ
   496έΗΤ- **Protocol**: OpenTelemetry
   497έΗΤ- **Exporters**: OTLP (configurable)
   498έΗΤ- **Spans**: API requests, service methods, DB queries, queue jobs
   499έΗΤ
   500έΗΤ### Metrics
   501έΗΤ
   502έΗΤ- **Endpoint**: `/metrics` (Prometheus format)
   503έΗΤ- **Metrics**: Request latency, queue depth, error rates, optimizer solve times
   504έΗΤ
   505έΗΤ### Health Checks
   506έΗΤ
   507έΗΤ- **Liveness**: `GET /health` (always 200 if process running)
   508έΗΤ- **Readiness**: `GET /ready` (checks DB + Redis connectivity)
   509έΗΤ
   510έΗΤ---
   511έΗΤ
   512έΗΤ## Security
   513έΗΤ
   514έΗΤ### Authentication
   515έΗΤ
   516έΗΤ- **Method**: JWT (Bearer tokens)
   517έΗΤ- **Storage**: HttpOnly cookies (frontend), Authorization header (API)
   518έΗΤ- **Refresh**: Sliding window with refresh tokens
   519έΗΤ
   520έΗΤ### Authorization
   521έΗΤ
   522έΗΤ- **Model**: Role-based (Admin, Planner, Viewer)
   523έΗΤ- **Enforcement**: Middleware on protected routes
   524έΗΤ
   525έΗΤ### API Security
   526έΗΤ
   527έΗΤ- **Rate limiting**: Per-IP and per-user limits
   528έΗΤ- **CORS**: Configured origins only
   529έΗΤ- **Input validation**: @belt/tools schemas on all endpoints
   530έΗΤ- **SQL injection**: Parameterized queries via Drizzle ORM
   531έΗΤ
   532έΗΤ### Container Security
   533έΗΤ
   534έΗΤ- **Non-root users**: All containers run as unprivileged
   535έΗΤ- **Read-only filesystem**: Where possible
   536έΗΤ- **Resource limits**: Memory and CPU constraints
   537έΗΤ
   538έΗΤ### Secrets Management
   539έΗΤ
   540έΗΤ- **Development**: `.env` file (gitignored)
   541έΗΤ- **Production**: Environment variables from orchestrator
   542έΗΤ
   543έΗΤ---
   544έΗΤ
   545έΗΤ## Common Tasks
   546έΗΤ
   547έΗΤ### Adding a New API Endpoint
   548έΗΤ
   549έΗΤ1. Create route in `src/api/routes/<domain>.ts`
   550έΗΤ2. Add @belt/tools schema for request/response
   551έΗΤ3. Implement service method in `src/services/<domain>.ts`
   552έΗΤ4. Add OpenAPI documentation
   553έΗΤ5. Write integration test
   554έΗΤ6. Mount route in `src/api/index.ts`
   555έΗΤ
   556έΗΤ### Adding a New Worker
   557έΗΤ
   558έΗΤ1. Create processor in `src/workers/processors/<name>.ts`
   559έΗΤ2. Define queue in `src/workers/queues.ts`
   560έΗΤ3. Register in `src/workers/runner.ts`
   561έΗΤ4. Add job enqueue method to relevant service
   562έΗΤ
   563έΗΤ### Adding a New Database Table
   564έΗΤ
   565έΗΤ1. Define schema in `src/lib/db/schema/<domain>.ts`
   566έΗΤ2. Export from `src/lib/db/schema/index.ts`
   567έΗΤ3. Run `bun run db:generate` to create migration
   568έΗΤ4. Run `bun run db:migrate` to apply
   569έΗΤ5. Create corresponding Drizzle queries
   570έΗΤ
   571έΗΤ---
   572έΗΤ
   573έΗΤ## Troubleshooting
   574έΗΤ
   575έΗΤ### Port Conflicts
   576έΗΤ
   577έΗΤ```bash
   578έΗΤlsof -i :5432  # PostgreSQL
   579έΗΤlsof -i :6379  # Redis
   580έΗΤlsof -i :3000  # API
   581έΗΤlsof -i :5173  # Frontend
   582έΗΤ```
   583έΗΤ
   584έΗΤ### Database Reset
   585έΗΤ
   586έΗΤ```bash
   587έΗΤdocker compose down -v
   588έΗΤdocker compose up -d postgres
   589έΗΤbun run db:migrate
   590έΗΤbun run db:seed  # Optional
   591έΗΤ```
   592έΗΤ
   593έΗΤ### Optimizer Not Found
   594έΗΤ
   595έΗΤ```bash
   596έΗΤ# Check binary exists
   597έΗΤls -la mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
   598έΗΤ
   599έΗΤ# Rebuild
   600έΗΤcd mms-optimizer && cmake --build build --clean-first
   601έΗΤ```
   602έΗΤ
   603έΗΤ### Worker Not Processing
   604έΗΤ
   605έΗΤ```bash
   606έΗΤ# Check Redis connection
   607έΗΤdocker compose logs redis
   608έΗΤ
   609έΗΤ# Check worker logs
   610έΗΤdocker compose logs worker
   611έΗΤ
   612έΗΤ# Verify queue has jobs
   613έΗΤ# Use Redis Commander at localhost:8081
   614έΗΤ```
   615έΗΤ
   616έΗΤ---
   617έΗΤ
   618έΗΤ## References
   619έΗΤ
   620έΗΤ- **Specifications**: `specs/001-009` (source of truth)
   621έΗΤ- **API Reference**: OpenAPI spec at `/api/v1/docs`
   622έΗΤ- **@belt/tools**: See `README.md` for complete API
   623έΗΤ- **Implementation Plan**: `ralph/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md`
   624έΗΤ- **Operational Guide**: `ralph/AGENTS.md`
   625έΗΤ

# MMS Architecture Documentation

**Metro Fleet Mileage Management System** - Constraint-based fleet management platform for train deployments and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year contract period.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [System Overview](#system-overview)
2. [Technology Stack](#technology-stack)
3. [Architecture Diagram](#architecture-diagram)
4. [Development Setup](#development-setup)
5. [Deployment](#deployment)
6. [Core Components](#core-components)
7. [Data Flow](#data-flow)
8. [Domain Model](#domain-model)
9. [Integration Points](#integration-points)
10. [Observability](#observability)
11. [Security](#security)

---

## System Overview

MMS is a full-stack application designed to manage preventive maintenance (PM) scheduling for metro train fleets. The system optimizes train-to-route assignments, tracks mileage, projects maintenance needs over a 12-year horizon, and provides an interactive canvas for planners to visualize and adjust schedules.

### Key Capabilities

- **Mileage Tracking**: Ingest daily mileage readings from CSV files, detect anomalies, handle odometer events
- **PM Scheduling**: Schedule preventive maintenance within compliance windows (C1έΗΤC2έΗΤC3έΗΤC4έΗΤOverhaul hierarchy)
- **Run Assignment Optimization**: Assign trains to routes balancing mileage across the fleet (C++ OR-Tools CP-SAT solver)
- **Strategic Planning**: 10-year maintenance projection using genetic algorithms
- **Man-hour Leveling**: Balance workforce across depots and time periods
- **Drift Analysis**: Compare actual vs. baseline to detect schedule deviation
- **Interactive Canvas**: WebGL-powered infinite canvas with semantic zoom (strategicέΗΤtacticalέΗΤoperational views)

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| **Runtime** | Bun 1.x |
| **Frontend** | React 18, Vite 7, deck.gl 9, Konva, BlueprintJS v6 |
| **Backend** | Hono 4.x (REST API + WebSocket) |
| **Database** | PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB 2.14 |
| **ORM** | Drizzle 0.44 |
| **Queue** | BullMQ 5.x (Redis-backed) |
| **Cache/Pub-Sub** | Redis 7 |
| **Optimizer** | C++ with OR-Tools CP-SAT (TypeScript fallback) |
| **Validation** | @belt/tools (TypeBox-based schemas) |
| **State Management** | @belt/tools machines (Immer-based) |
| **Observability** | OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Pino logging |
| **Containerization** | Docker, Docker Compose |

---

## Architecture Diagram

---

## Development Setup

### Prerequisites

- **Bun** 1.x (`curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash`)
- **Docker** & Docker Compose
- **Node.js** 20+ (for some tooling)
- **CMake** + OR-Tools (for C++ optimizer, optional)

### Quick Start

```bash
# Clone and install
git clone <repository>
cd mms/system
bun install

# Copy environment
cp .env.example .env
```

### Option 1: Hybrid Development (Recommended)

Run data services in Docker, application locally for hot-reload:

```bash
# Terminal 1: Start data services
docker compose up -d postgres redis

# Terminal 2: Run database migrations
bun run db:migrate

# Terminal 3: Start frontend (Vite dev server)
bun run dev
# έΗΤ http://localhost:5173

# Terminal 4: Start backend (Hono API)
bun run src/api/server.ts
# έΗΤ http://localhost:3000

# Terminal 5: Start workers (optional)
bun run src/workers/runner.ts
```

### Option 2: Full Docker Stack

Run everything in containers:

```bash
# Start all services
docker compose up -d

# With dev tools (pgAdmin, Redis Commander)
docker compose --profile tools up -d

# View logs
docker compose logs -f backend
docker compose logs -f worker
```

### Option 3: Minimal (Frontend Only)

For UI development without backend:

```bash
bun run dev
# Mock API responses or use storybook
```

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `POSTGRES_DB` | mms | Database name |
| `POSTGRES_USER` | mms | Database user |
| `POSTGRES_PASSWORD` | mms_dev_password | Database password |
| `POSTGRES_PORT` | 5432 | PostgreSQL port |
| `REDIS_PORT` | 6379 | Redis port |
| `API_PORT` | 3000 | Backend API port |
| `FRONTEND_PORT` | 5173 | Vite dev server port |
| `JWT_SECRET` | dev_jwt_secret... | JWT signing key |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | debug | Logging verbosity |
| `OTEL_ENABLED` | false | OpenTelemetry tracing |
| `WORKERS` | (all) | Comma-separated worker list |

### Database Commands

```bash
bun run db:generate    # Generate migration files from schema
bun run db:migrate     # Run pending migrations
bun run db:push        # Push schema directly (dev only)
bun run db:studio      # Open Drizzle Studio at localhost:4983
bun run db:seed        # Seed demo data
```

### C++ Optimizer Build (Optional)

```bash
# macOS prerequisites
brew install cmake or-tools nlohmann-json fmt

# Build
cd mms-optimizer
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build

# Verify
./build/mms-optimizer --version

# Set environment variable
export MMS_OPTIMIZER_BINARY=./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
```

### Validation Commands (Run in Order)

```bash
bun test <path>        # 1. Unit tests for changed files
bun run typecheck      # 2. TypeScript strict mode
bun run lint           # 3. ESLint
bun run build          # 4. Full production build
PORT=0 bun run test:integration  # 5. Integration tests
```

**All must pass before committing.**

---

## Deployment

### Docker Images

| Service | Dockerfile | Port | Description |
|---------|------------|------|-------------|
| backend | `docker/Dockerfile.backend` | 3000 | Hono API server |
| frontend | `docker/Dockerfile.frontend` | 80 | React SPA (Nginx) |
| worker | `docker/Dockerfile.worker` | - | BullMQ processors |
| file-watcher | `docker/Dockerfile.file-watcher` | - | CSV ingestion |
| optimizer | `mms-optimizer/Dockerfile` | - | C++ binary |

### Production Build

```bash
# Build all images
docker compose build

# Or individual
docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.backend -t mms-backend .
docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.frontend -t mms-frontend .
```

### Container Configuration

All containers:
- Run as non-root user
- Include health checks
- Use multi-stage builds for minimal size
- Target sizes: backend <200MB, frontend <50MB

### Docker Compose Services

```yaml
services:
  postgres:     # PostgreSQL 16 + TimescaleDB
  redis:        # Redis 7 Alpine
  backend:      # Hono API
  frontend:     # React/Vite (dev) or Nginx (prod)
  worker:       # BullMQ processors
  file-watcher: # CSV directory watcher
  optimizer:    # C++ OR-Tools binary
```

### Volumes

- `postgres_data` - Database persistence
- `redis_data` - Redis persistence
- `optimizer_binary` - Shared optimizer binary
- `file_inbox` / `file_quarantine` - CSV processing

### Network

All services communicate via `mms-network` bridge network.

---

### API Routes (`/api/v1`)

| Route | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `/auth` | Login, logout, token refresh |
| `/fleet` | Fleet and train management |
| `/trains` | Individual train CRUD |
| `/pm/schedule` | PM schedule operations |
| `/mileage` | Mileage readings, anomalies |
| `/optimizer` | Run assignment, tactical, strategic jobs |
| `/baselines` | Publish and query baselines |
| `/drift` | Drift analysis results |
| `/manhours` | Man-hour projections |
| `/scenarios` | What-if scenario management |
| `/canvas` | Pre-rendered canvas data |
| `/export` | Excel/PDF report generation |
| `/integration` | Maximo sync triggers |

### BullMQ Workers (9 queues)

| Queue | Concurrency | Description |
|-------|-------------|-------------|
| `ingestion` | 1 | CSV file processing (sequential) |
| `optimizer` | 5 | Run assignment optimization |
| `tactical_scheduler` | 1 | 12-week PM scheduling |
| `strategic_planner` | 1 | 10-year planning (GA) |
| `manhour_leveling` | 3 | Workforce optimization |
| `drift_monitor` | 3 | Baseline deviation analysis |
| `notification` | 5 | Email delivery |
| `report` | 3 | Excel/PDF generation |
| `maximo_sync` | 2 | Maximo push/pull/full sync |

## Data Flow

### Mileage Ingestion

```
CSV File Drop έΗΤ File Watcher έΗΤ Validation έΗΤ Queue (ingestion)
                                               έΦΓ
                                               έΨ╝
                              Parse έΗΤ Anomaly Detection έΗΤ mileage_readings
                                               έΦΓ
                                               έΨ╝
                              TimescaleDB έΗΤ Continuous Aggregates
```

### PM Scheduling

```
User Request έΗΤ API έΗΤ Queue (tactical_scheduler)
                           έΦΓ
                           έΨ╝
         Load Constraints έΗΤ Build Model έΗΤ Solve (OR-Tools or TS)
                           έΦΓ
                           έΨ╝
         pm_schedule έΗΤ WebSocket Broadcast έΗΤ UI Update
```

### Drift Analysis

```
Nightly Job έΗΤ Load Baseline έΗΤ Load Actual έΗΤ Compare
                                              έΦΓ
                                              έΨ╝
                              drift_analysis έΗΤ Flag Deviations
                                              έΦΓ
                                              έΨ╝
                              Notification έΗΤ Planner Alert
```

---

## Domain Model

### Core Entities

| Entity | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| **Fleet** | Group of trains (KS: Kinki Sharyo, ALSTOM: Metropolis) |
| **Train** | Individual vehicle with mileage, fitness status |
| **Depot** | Maintenance facility with tracks |
| **Track** | Physical location with capability (PIT, CRANE, etc.) |
| **Route** | Operational route with daily mileage profile |
| **PM Type** | Maintenance type (C1/C2/C3/C4/Overhaul) with intervals |
| **PM Schedule** | Scheduled maintenance event with compliance window |
| **Baseline** | Immutable snapshot for drift comparison |
| **Scenario** | What-if fork with delta events |

### PM Hierarchy

```
C1 (6,000 km / 3 months)
 έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ C2 (24,000 km / 6 months) έΦΑ satisfies C1
      έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ C3 (50,000 km / 12 months) έΦΑ satisfies C1, C2
           έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ C4 (200,000 km / 48 months) έΦΑ satisfies C1, C2, C3
                έΦΦέΦΑέΦΑ Overhaul (1,000,000 km / 144 months) έΦΑ satisfies all
```

### Compliance Windows

- **Early tolerance**: 10% before due date
- **Late tolerance**: 10% after due date
- **Hard deadline**: Statutory limit (train grounded if exceeded)

### Constraint Types

1. **Hard constraints** (NEVER violate): Safety limits, physical impossibilities
2. **Soft constraints** (violate with penalty): Compliance windows, preferences
3. **Material constraints**: Inventory availability
4. **Track constraints**: Capability matching

---

## Integration Points

### Maximo (CMMS)

- **Direction**: Bidirectional
- **Protocol**: REST API (OSLC)
- **Receives**: Corrective maintenance (CM) status, work order updates
- **Sends**: PM work order requests
- **Boundary**: MMS never manages CM work orders (Maximo-owned)

### File Inbox (Mileage CSV)

- **Direction**: Inbound only
- **Protocol**: Filesystem watch
- **Format**: CSV with train ID, date, mileage
- **Flow**: inbox έΗΤ processing έΗΤ archive/quarantine

### Inventory System

- **Direction**: Inbound only
- **Protocol**: Snapshot consumption
- **Data**: Material availability, reservation status

---

## Observability

### Logging

- **Library**: Pino (structured JSON)
- **Levels**: debug, info, warn, error
- **Context**: Request ID, user ID, trace ID

### Tracing

- **Protocol**: OpenTelemetry
- **Exporters**: OTLP (configurable)
- **Spans**: API requests, service methods, DB queries, queue jobs

### Metrics

- **Endpoint**: `/metrics` (Prometheus format)
- **Metrics**: Request latency, queue depth, error rates, optimizer solve times

### Health Checks

- **Liveness**: `GET /health` (always 200 if process running)
- **Readiness**: `GET /ready` (checks DB + Redis connectivity)

---

## Security

### Authentication

- **Method**: JWT (Bearer tokens)
- **Storage**: HttpOnly cookies (frontend), Authorization header (API)
- **Refresh**: Sliding window with refresh tokens

### Authorization

- **Model**: Role-based (Admin, Planner, Viewer)
- **Enforcement**: Middleware on protected routes

### API Security

- **Rate limiting**: Per-IP and per-user limits
- **CORS**: Configured origins only
- **Input validation**: @belt/tools schemas on all endpoints
- **SQL injection**: Parameterized queries via Drizzle ORM

### Container Security

- **Non-root users**: All containers run as unprivileged
- **Read-only filesystem**: Where possible
- **Resource limits**: Memory and CPU constraints

### Secrets Management

- **Development**: `.env` file (gitignored)
- **Production**: Environment variables from orchestrator

---

## Common Tasks

### Adding a New API Endpoint

1. Create route in `src/api/routes/<domain>.ts`
2. Add @belt/tools schema for request/response
3. Implement service method in `src/services/<domain>.ts`
4. Add OpenAPI documentation
5. Write integration test
6. Mount route in `src/api/index.ts`

### Adding a New Worker

1. Create processor in `src/workers/processors/<name>.ts`
2. Define queue in `src/workers/queues.ts`
3. Register in `src/workers/runner.ts`
4. Add job enqueue method to relevant service

### Adding a New Database Table

1. Define schema in `src/lib/db/schema/<domain>.ts`
2. Export from `src/lib/db/schema/index.ts`
3. Run `bun run db:generate` to create migration
4. Run `bun run db:migrate` to apply
5. Create corresponding Drizzle queries

---

## Troubleshooting

### Port Conflicts

```bash
lsof -i :5432  # PostgreSQL
lsof -i :6379  # Redis
lsof -i :3000  # API
lsof -i :5173  # Frontend
```

### Database Reset

```bash
docker compose down -v
docker compose up -d postgres
bun run db:migrate
bun run db:seed  # Optional
```

### Optimizer Not Found

```bash
# Check binary exists
ls -la mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer

# Rebuild
cd mms-optimizer && cmake --build build --clean-first
```

### Worker Not Processing

```bash
# Check Redis connection
docker compose logs redis

# Check worker logs
docker compose logs worker

# Verify queue has jobs
# Use Redis Commander at localhost:8081
```

file.content:
# MMS Backend Service
# Multi-stage build for Bun + Hono API server
#
# Build:
#   docker build -f docker/Dockerfile.backend -t mms-backend .
#
# Run:
#   docker run -p 3000:3000 -e DATABASE_URL=... -e REDIS_URL=... mms-backend
#
# Size target: <200MB
# @see specs/008-operations.md Section 8.2.2

# =============================================================================
# Stage 1: Dependencies
# =============================================================================
FROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine AS dependencies

WORKDIR /app

# Copy dependency files
COPY package.json bun.lockb ./

# Install production dependencies only
RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile --production

# =============================================================================
# Stage 2: Builder
# =============================================================================
FROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /app

# Copy package files and install all dependencies
COPY package.json bun.lockb ./
RUN bun install --frozen-lockfile

# Copy source code
COPY tsconfig.json ./
COPY src ./src
COPY drizzle ./drizzle

# Type check and build
RUN bun run typecheck

# =============================================================================
# Stage 3: Production
# =============================================================================
FROM oven/bun:1.1-alpine AS production

WORKDIR /app

# Add non-root user for security
RUN addgroup -S mms && adduser -S mms -G mms

# Copy production dependencies
COPY --from=dependencies /app/node_modules ./node_modules

# Copy application code
COPY --from=builder /app/package.json ./
COPY --from=builder /app/src ./src
COPY --from=builder /app/drizzle ./drizzle

# Set ownership
RUN chown -R mms:mms /app

# Switch to non-root user
USER mms

# Environment
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOST=0.0.0.0

# Expose port
EXPOSE 3000

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget --no-verbose --tries=1 --spider http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1

# Start server
CMD ["bun", "run", "src/api/server.ts"]

file.content:
# MMS Manual Database Migrations

These SQL migrations configure PostgreSQL/TimescaleDB features that Drizzle ORM doesn't handle automatically.

## Migration Order

Run these migrations **after** Drizzle-generated schema migrations:

```bash
# 1. Generate and run Drizzle migrations (creates tables)
bun drizzle-kit generate
bun drizzle-kit migrate

# 2. Run manual migrations in order
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/0001_extensions.sql
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/0002_timescaledb.sql
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/0003_functions.sql
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/0004_materialized_views.sql

# 3. (Optional) Seed development data
psql $DATABASE_URL -f drizzle/manual/seed_development.sql
```

## Migration Files

| File | Purpose | Prerequisites |
|------|---------|---------------|
| `0001_extensions.sql` | Enable uuid-ossp and TimescaleDB extensions | Superuser privileges |
| `0002_timescaledb.sql` | Configure hypertable, compression, retention | Extensions, mileage_readings table |
| `0003_functions.sql` | Database functions and triggers | Schema tables |
| `0004_materialized_views.sql` | Canvas optimization views | Schema tables, functions |
| `seed_development.sql` | Sample data for development | All migrations |

## Requirements

- PostgreSQL 16+
- TimescaleDB 2.x extension installed
- Superuser access for extension creation

## Verification

Each migration includes verification checks. Look for:
- `NOTICE: ... verified successfully` = OK
- `WARNING: ...` = Non-critical issue
- `EXCEPTION: ...` = Migration failed

## Rollback

Manual rollback scripts are not provided. For rollback:

1. Drop materialized views: `DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW IF EXISTS v_canvas_* CASCADE;`
2. Drop functions: `DROP FUNCTION IF EXISTS <name> CASCADE;`
3. Drop hypertable config: TimescaleDB config is bound to table (requires table drop)
4. Drop extensions: `DROP EXTENSION IF EXISTS timescaledb CASCADE;`

**Warning**: Dropping TimescaleDB extension will drop all hypertables and continuous aggregates.

  - Installed dependencies via Homebrew: `cmake`, `or-tools`, `nlohmann-json`, `fmt`
  - Fixed unused parameter warnings in `solver.cpp` (lines 574, 655)
  - Fixed lambda capture issue in `main.cpp` (line 142 - static variable `g_requestId`)
  - Compiled binary at `mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer` (418KB)
  - Updated TypeScript wrapper default path to `./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer`
  - Added `mms-optimizer/build` to eslint ignore patterns
  - Binary tested with simple optimization problem: returns OPTIMAL solution with correct mileage balancing
- **All Validations Pass**: typecheck, lint, build, 1695 tests
- **Remaining Minor Issues** (documented, not blocking):
  - Soft constraint breakdown uses hardcoded ratios (80%/20%) instead of actual solver values
  - `summary.materialBlocks = 0` placeholder, not counted from warnings
  - `test/` directory missing for C++ unit tests

### GAP-2: Report Worker Placeholder Implementation (P2 - HIGH) έεΖ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/workers/processors/report.ts`
- **Status**: έεΖ RESOLVED - All data fetching functions implemented with actual database queries and service integrations
- **Implementation**:
  - `fetchPmScheduleData()` - Queries pm_schedule table with joins to trains, fleets, pmTypes, depots, tracks
  - `fetchFleetStatusData()` - Uses FleetService.getAllFleetStatistics() for fleet metrics
  - `fetchDriftReportData()` - Uses DriftMonitorService.runDriftAnalysis() for drift data
  - `fetchTcoProjectionData()` - Uses CostingService.projectTCO() for TCO projections
  - ExcelJS integrated for proper .xlsx generation with formatting and metadata sheets
  - pdfmake integrated for PDF generation with structured layouts, tables, and summaries
- **Dependencies Added**: exceljs@4.4.0, pdfmake@0.3.3, @types/pdfmake@0.2.12
- **All validations pass** (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-3: /metrics Endpoint NOT EXPOSED (P2 - HIGH) έεΖ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/api/index.ts`
- **Status**: έεΖ RESOLVED - `/metrics` endpoint added returning Prometheus text format
- **Implementation**:
  - Added `GET /metrics` endpoint in `src/api/index.ts:132-137`
  - Returns `getMetrics()` with `text/plain; version=0.0.4` content type
  - 8 unit tests added in `src/api/index.test.ts`
  - All validations pass (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-4: Health Check Returns Hardcoded Values (P2 - HIGH) έεΖ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/api/index.ts:117-163`
- **Status**: έεΖ RESOLVED - /ready endpoint now performs actual health checks
- **Implementation**:
  - Uses `checkDatabaseHealth()` from `src/lib/db/connection.ts`
  - Uses `checkRedisHealth()` from `src/lib/redis/connection.ts`
  - 5-second timeout for each health check
  - Returns 200 if all services healthy, 503 if any unhealthy
  - 4 new unit tests for healthy/unhealthy scenarios
  - All validations pass (typecheck, lint, build)

### GAP-5: Database Missing Generated Columns (P2 - MEDIUM) έεΖ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Status**: έεΖ RESOLVED - All 8 generated columns implemented using Drizzle `.generatedAlwaysAs()`
- **Tables Updated**:
  - `trains.mileage_variance_km` = `current_mileage_km - COALESCE(target_mileage_km, current_mileage_km)`
  - `inventory_positions.quantity_available` = `quantity_on_hand - quantity_reserved`
  - `manhour_projections.variance_hours` = `available_total_hours - required_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.utilization_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
  - `manhour_projections.is_overloaded` = `required_total_hours > available_total_hours`
  - `manhour_projections.is_underutilized` = `required_total_hours < available_total_hours * 0.7`
  - `drift_analysis.mileage_drift_km` = `actual_km - baseline_projected_km`
  - `cost_baselines.variance_pct` = percentage calculation with null safety
- **Benefit**: Computed at database level ensures consistency, simplifies queries, enables indexing

### GAP-6: Database Missing notification_templates Table (P2 - MEDIUM) έεΖ RESOLVED (2026-01-20)
- **Location**: `src/lib/db/schema/notifications.ts`
- **Status**: έεΖ RESOLVED - `notification_templates` table implemented with full template management

# MMS Operational Guide

## Quick Reference

```bash
# Full stack (dev) - all services via Docker
cp .env.example .env
docker compose up -d

# Hybrid (data in Docker, app local)
docker compose up -d postgres redis
bun install
bun run dev                # Frontend
bun run src/api/server.ts  # Backend (separate terminal)

# Tests
bun test                    # Unit tests
bun test:watch             # Watch mode
bun run test:integration   # Integration (needs Docker)

# Validation (run in order!)
bun run typecheck          # TypeScript strict
bun run lint               # ESLint
bun run build              # Full build
```

## Validation Order (Backpressure)

**IMPORTANT: Run these in order after implementing changes:**

```bash
# 1. Unit tests for changed files
bun test <path-to-changed-file>

# 2. TypeScript type checking
bun run typecheck

# 3. Linting
bun run lint

# 4. Full build
bun run build

# 5. Integration tests (if touching API/DB/services/workers)
PORT=0 bun run test:integration

# 6. E2E via Playwright MCP (if UI modified)
# Use mcp__playwright__* tools directly - see PROMPT_build.md step 103
```

**Port Configuration:**
- Always use `PORT=0` for random port assignment
- Parse actual port from server startup output
- Never hardcode ports in tests

All must pass before committing. 12-year contract demands production-grade quality gates.

## Build & Run

### Development Environment

```bash
# Start infrastructure
docker-compose up -d postgres redis minio

# Install dependencies
bun install

# Run migrations
bun run db:migrate

# Seed demo data (optional)
bun run db:seed

# Start dev server
bun run dev
```

### C++ Optimizer Build

```bash
# Prerequisites (macOS)
brew install cmake or-tools nlohmann-json fmt

# Build
cd mms-optimizer
cmake -B build -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release
cmake --build build

# Binary location: mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer
# Environment variable: MMS_OPTIMIZER_BINARY (default: ./mms-optimizer/build/mms-optimizer)
```

### Docker Full Stack

```bash
# Copy environment example
cp .env.example .env

# Start all services

1έΗΤ# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
     2έΗΤ# Part 6: User Interface Specification
     3έΗΤ
     4έΗΤ**Document Version:** 2.0  
     5έΗΤ**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT
     6έΗΤ
     7έΗΤ---
     8έΗΤ
     9έΗΤ## 6.1 Design System Overview
    10έΗΤ
    11έΗΤ### 6.1.1 Technology Stack
    12έΗΤ
    13έΗΤ| Component | Technology | Purpose |
    14έΗΤ|-----------|------------|---------|
    15έΗΤ| Framework | React 18 | Component architecture |
    16έΗΤ| State | Zustand | Global state management |
    17έΗΤ| Canvas | deck.gl | WebGL timeline rendering |
    18έΗΤ| Components | BlueprintJS v6 | UI component library |
    19έΗΤ| Styling | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first styling |
    20έΗΤ| Build | Vite | Development and bundling |
    21έΗΤ| Types | TypeScript | Type safety |
    22έΗΤ
    23έΗΤ### 6.1.2 Design Principles
    24έΗΤ
    25έΗΤ1. **Information Density**: Maximize data visibility without overwhelming
    26έΗΤ2. **Semantic Zoom**: Data representation adapts to zoom level
    27έΗΤ3. **Direct Manipulation**: Drag-and-drop with immediate feedback
    28έΗΤ4. **Progressive Disclosure**: Details on demand
    29έΗΤ5. **Constraint Visibility**: Show limits, not just violations
    30έΗΤ6. **Explainability**: Every system decision has visible rationale
    31έΗΤ

    60έΗΤ

    64έΗΤ    optimal: '#10B981',      // Green
    65έΗΤ    underutilized: '#3B82F6', // Blue
    66έΗΤ  },
    67έΗΤ  
    68έΗΤ  // Fleet Colors

    73έΗΤ};

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 6: User Interface Specification

**Document Version:** 2.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT

---

## 6.1 Design System Overview

### 6.1.1 Technology Stack

| Component | Technology | Purpose |
|-----------|------------|---------|
| Framework | React 18 | Component architecture |
| State | Zustand | Global state management |
| Canvas | deck.gl | WebGL timeline rendering |
| Components | BlueprintJS v6 | UI component library |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first styling |
| Build | Vite | Development and bundling |
| Types | TypeScript | Type safety |

### 6.1.2 Design Principles

1. **Information Density**: Maximize data visibility without overwhelming
2. **Semantic Zoom**: Data representation adapts to zoom level
3. **Direct Manipulation**: Drag-and-drop with immediate feedback
4. **Progressive Disclosure**: Details on demand
5. **Constraint Visibility**: Show limits, not just violations
6. **Explainability**: Every system decision has visible rationale

### 6.1.3 Color System

```typescript
const colors = {
  // PM Status
  pm: {
    scheduled: '#3B82F6',    // Blue
    inProgress: '#F59E0B',   // Amber
    completed: '#10B981',    // Green
    cancelled: '#6B7280',    // Gray
    deferred: '#8B5CF6',     // Purple
    overdue: '#EF4444',      // Red
  },
  
  // Constraint Indicators
  constraints: {
    hardViolation: '#DC2626',    // Red-600
    softViolation: '#F59E0B',    // Amber-500
    approaching: '#FBBF24',      // Yellow-400
    ok: '#10B981',               // Green-500
  },
  
  // Drift Severity
  drift: {
    critical: '#DC2626',     // Red
    warning: '#F59E0B',      // Amber
    ok: '#10B981',           // Green
  },
  
  // Utilization
  utilization: {
    overloaded: '#DC2626',   // Red
    optimal: '#10B981',      // Green
    underutilized: '#3B82F6', // Blue
  },
  
  // Fleet Colors
  fleets: {
    KS: '#0EA5E9',           // Sky-500
    ALSTOM: '#8B5CF6',       // Violet-500
  }
};
```

---

# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
# Part 8: Operations & Deployment

**Document Version:** 2.0  
**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT

---

## 8.1 Deployment Architecture

| Staging | Integration testing | Kubernetes (single node) | Anonymized prod copy |
